theme: container-crane and AGV scheduling in a yard
title: Container Yard: Corridor Reservation Dispatch
strategies: overcautious avoidance: take the shortest path but refuse any move whose route touches a single-lane shortcut at all, forfeiting its throughput even when no other AGV would ever contend for it

uncoordinated shortest-path racing: every AGV departs immediately down its graph-shortest path with no awareness of others, so two AGVs entering the same shortcut from opposite ends at overlapping times meet head-on -- a deadlock that jams the corridor and strands every later move that still needed it

space-time corridor reservation: dispatch moves in release order while booking each shortcut's committed occupancy windows in a table; delay entry into a shortcut until a free window opens (or take the longer ring-only detour when that finishes sooner than waiting), so no two AGVs ever occupy the same shortcut at once and no head-on can occur
reservation is what lets a coordinator recover the moves an uncoordinated dispatch wipes out via cascading corridor jams -- at the modest cost of a little wait or a longer detour
